title: React Forms Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of controlled components, input handling, and form submission in React.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary purpose of a 'Controlled Component' in React?
options: To allow HTML to manage its own state.
To make React state the 'single source of truth' for form input values.
To hide inputs from the user.
To prevent any user from submitting forms.

questions.question: Which attribute should you use to link an input's displayed value to a piece of React state?
options: displayedValue
content
value
stateValue

questions.question: Which event handler is most commonly used to update the state as a user types into an input field?
options: onInput
onKeyUp
onChange
onClick

questions.question: How do you handle multiple inputs using a single onChange handler?
options: By using different functions for each input.
By using the 'name' attribute on each input and computed property names in the state object.
By using a global 'value' variable for all inputs.
It's not possible.

questions.question: Can you use controlled component patterns with <textarea> and <select> elements in React?
options: Yes, they behave similarly to <input> in React.
No, they are for different purposes.
Only in older versions of React.
Only by using ref instead of value.
